rulings.Case Title: CONNECTED HOME LIVING, INC. VS. HOME CARE FOR ADULTS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Request For Reconsideration And For Relief Of Entry Of Dismissal Without Prejudice; Request For Further Hearing For Prove Up Of Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 04, 2025, line 1, PLAINTIFF CONNECTED HOME LIVING, INC.'S Request For Reconsideration And For Relief Of Entry Of Dismissal Without Prejudice; Request For Further Hearing For Prove Up Of Default Judgment Off calendar. Please contact department 505 and re-calendar the motion before Judge Tong, who issued the dismissal. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN LUO VS. MICHELLE IKUE SOU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 04, 2025, line 2, DEFENDANT IKUE SOU MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND The unopposed motion to set aside and vacate the default entered against defendant Ikue Michelle Sou is granted. The default is set aside and defendant shall respond to the operative complaint within 20 days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: THE PARTY STAFF, INC. VS. QWICK, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 04, 2025, line 6 3 - DEFENDANT GARUDA LABS, INC. D/B/A INSTAWORK DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendants Aramark Corp., Guckenheimer Enterprises, Inc., and Guckenheimer Holdings, LLC ("Moving Defendants") demur to plaintiff the Party Staff, Inc.'s First Amended Complaint (FAC). For the reasons stated herein, the court sustains the demurrer with leave to amend as to the Unfair Competition Law (UCL) and Unfair Practices Act (UPA) causes of action and without leave to amend as to the tortious interference cause of action. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)
